existing_Entity_Description: Westbank First Nation (WFN) is a unincorporated community in Okanagan, British Columbia, Canada, within the Central Okanagan. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Westbank First Nation (WFN) had a population of 9,500. The community is situated in BC's sun-drenched wine country with stunning lake scenery and resort-style livin. a rapidly growing region with strong tourism and retirement appeal. The region features hot, dry summers and snowy winters — a four-season playground.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Semi-arid interior climate: hot dry summers regularly topping 35°C, cold but comparatively dry winters, and one of Canada's longest growing seasons; wildfire smoke and interface fire risk are annual realities; irrigation is essential.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: A chain of long lakes (Okanagan, Skaha, Osoyoos) in a dry valley between the Thompson Plateau and Monashee ranges; benchland terraces of orchards and vineyards above the lakeshore.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Canada's premier wine region (180+ wineries) and historic orchard belt; Kelowna is BC's largest interior city with UBC Okanagan, Kelowna International Airport, tech and healthcare; tourism and retirement migration drive growth.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Lakeshore and view homes, vineyard and orchard acreages, fast-built post-2000 suburbs (West Kelowna, Lake Country), and older single-storey homes in Penticton and Vernon; stucco and dry-climate exteriors dominate.
